summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates AWS Secrets Manager contents to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes `/bin/sh -c` to pip-install boto3 and run an inline Python heredoc that enumerates AWS Secrets Manager across us-east-1, eu-west-1, and eu-central-1 (via boto3 list_secrets/get_secret_value plus the aws CLI), then PUTs the harvested secret contents as JSON over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare IP at http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-472839 (with http://127.0.0.1:30782 as a secondary target). The script gates execution on the presence of AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, a podman container marker, and cwd /app/, narrowing the trigger to specific build/CI environments. There is no documented purpose, no TLS, and the destination is a bare IP unrelated to any legitimate publisher — an unambiguous install-time credential-theft and exfiltration channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
